questions.text: The peak value of an AC current is 5A. Its RMS value is:
questions.explanation: The RMS value (I_rms) is related to the peak value (I_0) by the relation I_rms = I_0 / √2.

questions.text: In a purely resistive AC circuit, the phase difference between current and voltage is:
questions.explanation: In a resistor, voltage and current are always in phase.

questions.text: In a purely inductive circuit, the current ________ the voltage by π/2.
questions.explanation: For a pure inductor, current lags behind the voltage by a phase angle of 90 degrees (π/2).

questions.text: The inductive reactance (X_L) of an inductor of inductance L in an AC circuit of frequency f is:
questions.explanation: X_L = ωL = 2πfL. It is directly proportional to frequency.

questions.text: In a purely capacitive circuit, the current ________ the voltage by π/2.
questions.explanation: For a pure capacitor, current leads the voltage by a phase angle of 90 degrees (π/2).

questions.text: The capacitive reactance (X_C) of a capacitor C for a DC source is:
questions.explanation: For DC, frequency f = 0. X_C = 1/(2πfC) = 1/0 = infinite. A capacitor blocks DC.

questions.text: The average power dissipated in a pure inductor or pure capacitor over a full cycle is:
questions.explanation: Since the phase difference is π/2, the power factor cos(π/2) = 0, so average power is zero.

questions.text: The impedance (Z) of a series LCR circuit is given by:
questions.explanation: Impedance is the total opposition; it's the phasor sum of resistance and net reactance.

questions.text: At resonance in a series LCR circuit, the impedance is:
questions.explanation: At resonance, X_L = X_C, so Z = R, which is its minimum possible value.

questions.text: The resonant frequency (f_r) of a series LCR circuit is:
questions.explanation: From ω_r = 1/√LC and f = ω/2π, we get f_r = 1/(2π√LC).

questions.text: The power factor of an LCR circuit is defined as:
questions.explanation: Power factor is the ratio of real power to apparent power, equal to cos φ = R/Z.

questions.text: A wattless current flows in a circuit when the phase difference between V and I is:
questions.explanation: When φ = 90°, cos φ = 0, and no power is consumed despite current flow; this is wattless current.

questions.text: Quality factor (Q-factor) of an LCR circuit represents:
questions.explanation: Q = (ω_r L) / R. Higher Q means a sharper resonance curve and better selectivity.

questions.text: A transformer works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Transformers transfer electrical energy between circuits through a varying magnetic field (mutual induction).

questions.text: In a step-up transformer, the number of turns in the secondary coil (N_s) compared to the primary (N_p) is:
questions.explanation: A step-up transformer increases voltage, which requires more turns in the secondary coil.

questions.text: Energy losses in a transformer include:
questions.explanation: Energy is lost due to resistance in wires, magnetic leakage, and heating of the core.

questions.text: The core of a transformer is laminated to reduce:
questions.explanation: Lamination breaks the paths of eddy currents, reducing heat dissipation in the core.

questions.text: In an AC generator, the induced EMF is maximum when the plane of the coil is ________ to the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: When parallel, the rate of change of magnetic flux linkage is maximum.

questions.text: The ratio of peak voltage to peak current in an AC circuit is called:
questions.explanation: Impedance is the effective resistance of an electric circuit or component to alternating current.

questions.text: If the frequency of an AC source is doubled, the capacitive reactance becomes:
questions.explanation: X_C = 1/(2πfC). Since f is in the denominator, doubling f halves X_C.

questions.text: The phase relationship between voltage across L and C in a series LCR circuit is:
questions.explanation: V_L leads I by 90°, V_C lags I by 90°. Thus, V_L and V_C are 180° (π) out of phase.

questions.text: LC oscillations involve the exchange of energy between:
questions.explanation: Energy oscillates between the electric field of the capacitor and the magnetic field of the inductor.

questions.text: If V = V_0 sin(ωt) and I = I_0 sin(ωt - π/2), the circuit element is a:
questions.explanation: The current lags voltage by π/2, which is the characteristic of a pure inductor.

questions.text: For a series resonant circuit, the power factor is:
questions.explanation: At resonance, φ = 0, so cos φ = cos 0 = 1.

questions.text: The unit of Reactance and Impedance is:
questions.explanation: Since they both represent opposition to current flow, their SI unit is the Ohm (Ω).

questions.text: In a series LCR circuit, if X_L > X_C, the circuit is ________ in nature.
questions.explanation: If inductive reactance dominates, the overall behavior of the circuit is inductive.

questions.text: The frequency of AC in India is:
questions.explanation: The standard domestic supply frequency in India is 50 cycles per second.

questions.text: The transformation ratio (k) of a transformer is given by:
questions.explanation: For an ideal transformer, the ratio of turns equals the ratio of voltages and inverse ratio of currents.

questions.text: Hard magnetic materials are used for transformer cores to reduce:
questions.explanation: Soft magnetic materials like soft iron are used to *minimize* hysteresis loss.

questions.text: The RMS voltage of domestic supply is 220V. Its peak voltage is approximately:
In an LCR circuit, the voltage across R, L, and C are 3V, 4V, and 8V. The total voltage is:
Sharpness of resonance increases when:
questions.explanation: Quality factor Q = ωL/R. Decreasing R increases Q, resulting in a sharper peak.

questions.text: AC is preferred over DC for long distance transmission because:
options: Voltage can be stepped up to reduce line losses
questions.explanation: High voltage transmission reduces current, which minimizes I²R heat losses in power lines.

questions.text: The power factor of a purely inductive circuit is:
The ratio of RMS value to the average value of AC for a half cycle is:
questions.explanation: Form factor = RMS / Average = (I_0/√2) / (2I_0/π) = π / (2√2) ≈ 1.11.

questions.text: A choke coil is used in fluorescent tubes to:
questions.explanation: An inductor (choke) limits current with very low power dissipation compared to a resistor.

questions.text: If a capacitor is connected to a DC circuit, after a long time it acts as a/an:
questions.explanation: Once fully charged, the capacitor blocks further DC flow, acting as an open break in the circuit.

questions.text: In an ideal transformer, input power is ________ output power.
questions.explanation: An ideal transformer is assumed to have 100% efficiency with no energy losses.

questions.text: If the current in an AC circuit is given by I = sin(100πt), the frequency is:
The instantaneous power in an AC circuit is given by P = VI. The average power is P_avg = V_rms * I_rms * cos φ. The term 'cos φ' is:
questions.explanation: This is the definition of the power factor.
